your_book:
Friday was broadly soft for Jaleel's book, with 13 of 18 holdings closing in the red and the portfolio sitting on an unrealized loss of $212 (-0.98%) across a $21,546 account. The brightest spot was Abbott Laboratories, which added 1.87% on the day and remains the strongest open gain in the book at +16.4% — STRONG BUY conviction intact and the EPS guide trim flagged as a yellow, not red, flag. Adobe also nudged up 0.82%, a modest positive given the persistent AI-disruption narrative weighing on its multiple. On the other side, NVIDIA slid 2.21% as export-control headlines and open-source AI commentary from Chamath Palihapitiya kept sentiment skittish — the stock is still +10.5% open, but the risk flag is active with earnings due August 25. Uber fell 2.13%; the Delivery Hero acquisition news dominated the week's consumer-tech tape and introduces integration uncertainty worth tracking.

The clearest thesis-vs-price divergences remain in Rocket Lab and Boston Scientific. RKLB is down 26.1% from cost and carries an AVOID verdict with a QARP of 44.6 — the weakest score in the book by a wide margin; Friday's +0.4% does nothing to close that gap. BSX has a STRONG BUY verdict and an 82.7 QARP yet sits -8.0% open after guidance was slashed to 7–8.5% growth with management guiding flat through Q3 — that's the clearest case of conviction colliding with deteriorating fundamentals, and earnings land July 29. Lululemon rounds out the concern cluster: -9.0% open, a guidance cut in effect, Americas comps down five straight quarters, and Friday's -2.07% session loss. Its next update isn't until September 2, so the position is sitting in a holding pattern with elevated risk and little near-term catalyst.

Worth watching into next week: ServiceNow reports July 22 — it's the second-largest position at $2,375 and carries the concentration-risk flag. Coca-Cola's -3.96% drop on Friday stood out as the session's worst single-day move in the book; the headline driver appears to be UK energy-drink regulation news bleeding across the beverage space, which is a limited read-through for KO but worth verifying. Cash is effectively zero ($0.38), so any repositioning requires selling something first.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, -26.1% open, lowest QARP in book at 44.6
attention.note: Rocket Lab is the only AVOID in the portfolio with a score 20+ points below the next-weakest holding — Jaleel's book has no thesis here that the data supports, and the open loss is the deepest in the portfolio.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, earnings July 29 — conviction vs. fundamentals at odds
attention.note: A STRONG BUY/82.7 QARP position is sitting -8.0% open after FY26 growth was cut to 7–8.5% with management flagging flat performance through Q3; the next print in 10 days will either validate holding or force a reassessment.

attention.headline: Largest weight reports July 22 — macro beta risk in focus
attention.note: At 11.0% of the book, ServiceNow's earnings in days are the single biggest near-term event risk; a high-multiple software name with a hawkish-Fed de-rate flag already active.

attention.headline: Fifth straight negative Americas comp, -9.0% open, no catalyst until September 2
attention.note: Friday's -2.07% session loss adds to an already deteriorating picture — guidance cut in place, margins pressured by tariffs, and China stalled, with six weeks until the next update and no floor in sight.
